meaning: n.海军上将职位,舰队司令职位 (Admiralty)n. (英国)海军部
examples: The eighteenth-century British Admiralty had few coercive powers over its officers.
He noted the latitude and longitude, then made a mark on the admiralty chart.
The accompanying table is derived from Admiralty statistics for 1914 and 1928.
One of the letters discovered by archivists was from Lord Melville, First Lord of the Admiralty.
The area from Admiralty Arch to the Queen Victoria Memorial will be ticketed and reserved.
